title: Polygala phlebotaenia
taxonomy: Polygala phlebotaenia is a species of flowering plant in the family Polygalaceae, commonly known as the milkwort family. This plant is native to tropical Africa and is characterized by its unique flowers and foliage. Polygala phlebotaenia is a member of the genus Polygala, which comprises over 500 species of plants. The genus Polygala is further classified under the order Polygalales, a group of flowering plants that are often found in tropical and subtropical regions. intro: Polygala phlebotaenia, commonly known as 'Fleeceflower', is a perennial plant species belonging to the Polygalaceae family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiovascular-protective effects. The bioactive constituents of Polygala phlebotaenia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
